title: Mattermost Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Mattermost** is an open-source, self-hostable team messaging platform (Slack-equivalent) exposing a REST API under `{server_url}/api/v4`. Unlike SaaS APIs, the base URL is **tenant-specific** — every Mattermost deployment has its own hostname. This connector — `MattermostConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "mattermost"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from a Mattermost deployment:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| System | `/system/ping` | Liveness probe (no auth required) |
| Users | `/users` | Get me, list/get/create users, search |
| Teams | `/teams` | List + read teams the token has access to |
| Channels | `/channels`, `/teams/{id}/channels` | List + read + create + delete channels, manage members |
| Posts | `/posts` | Create / read / update / delete posts; threaded replies; channel timeline |
| Files | `/files` | Upload + download + metadata for attachments |
| Webhooks | `/hooks/incoming`, `/hooks/outgoing` | Create / list incoming + outgoing webhooks |
| Bots | `/bots` | Provision bot accounts and list active bots |
| Commands | `/commands` | List slash-commands installed on a team |

The connector normalises posts + channels + users into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff that honours `Retry-After` / `X-RateLimit-Reset` headers, and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::MattermostHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Generate a Personal Access Token

1. Open your Mattermost server in a browser.
2. Go to **Account Settings → Security → Personal Access Tokens**. If the section is missing, your system admin must first enable PATs in **System Console → Integrations → Integration Management → Enable Personal Access Tokens**.
3. Click **Create Token**, enter a description (e.g. `Shielva Integration`).
4. Copy the **Access Token** value — Mattermost shows it only once.
5. Note your server's base URL (e.g. `https://mattermost.acme.com`). Do NOT include the `/api/v4` suffix — the connector adds it automatically.
6. Optionally copy the 26-char ID of the team you want to default to from the URL (`/<team-name>/channels/...`).

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Mattermost**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `server_url` (text) | `https://mattermost.acme.com` |
| `personal_access_token` (secret) | Token from step 4 |
| `default_team_id` (text, optional) | Team ID — used as a default when methods accept one |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number) | Soft cap, default 200 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `server_url` + `personal_access_token` are non-empty, probes `GET /users/me`, persists the config, stores the token (`set_token`), and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, CONNECTED)`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway periodically calls `health_check()`, which probes `GET /system/ping` (the canonical Mattermost liveness probe). On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 → `DEGRADED + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. On 404 → `OFFLINE + FAILED` (wrong server URL). On 429 → `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` with structured-log warning.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Mattermost REST API uses **server-to-server Personal Access Token (PAT) authentication** (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`). No OAuth dance, no refresh, no expiry — tokens are valid until revoked.

### Credential model

- `personal_access_token` is sent in the `Authorization` header as `Bearer <token>` (standard Mattermost convention).
- `server_url` defines the base. The connector strips trailing `/` and any accidental `/api/v4` suffix at install time via `helpers/utils.py::normalize_server_url`.
- Bot account tokens (provisioned via `/bots`) work identically — same header, same lifecycle.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `server_url` + `personal_access_token`. Probes `GET /users/me`. Stores config + token. |
| `authorize(...)` | Re-probes `/users/me` and returns a `TokenInfo` carrying the stored PAT (lifecycle parity with OAuth connectors). |
| `health_check()` | `GET /system/ping` — lightweight liveness probe. |
| `ensure_token()` | N/A (PATs do not expire unless revoked). |
| token storage | `set_token()` writes the PAT into `BaseConnector` token storage. |

### Header contract

Every outbound request:

```
Authorization: Bearer <personal_access_token>
Content-Type:  application/json
Accept:        application/json
```

File uploads (`POST /files`) drop `Content-Type` so httpx can inject the multipart boundary.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `server_url` | string | yes | `self.server_url` | Tenant-specific base URL — `https://mm.example.com` |
| `personal_access_token` | secret | yes | `self.personal_access_token` | `Authorization: Bearer <PAT>` |
| `default_team_id` | string | no | `self.default_team_id` | Optional default team for shorthand calls |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 200) | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
CONNECTOR_TYPE = "mattermost"
AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"
VERSION = "1.0.0"
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["server_url", "personal_access_token"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
```

Mutations to `server_url` or `personal_access_token` should call `_rebuild_http_client()` so the in-flight `MattermostHTTPClient` picks up the new credentials.

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `MattermostConnector`. SOC: `connector.py` orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::MattermostHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `server_url` + `personal_access_token`; probe `/users/me`; persist config + token.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
Re-probes `/users/me` and returns a TokenInfo carrying the PAT.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Probes `GET /system/ping`. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Enumerates teams → channels → recent channel posts (one page each), normalizes each via `helpers/normalizer.py`, and ingests via `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_synced / _failed / _found`.

children.title: Users
children.content:
### `async get_me() -> Dict`  (alias: `get_current_user`)
`GET /users/me`.

### `async list_users(*, page=0, per_page=60, in_team_id=None, in_channel_id=None) -> List[Dict]`
`GET /users?page=&per_page=&in_team=&in_channel=`.

### `async get_user(user_id) -> Dict`
`GET /users/{user_id}`.

### `async create_user(payload) -> Dict`
`POST /users` (admin-only).

### `async search_users(team_id, term, *, in_channel_id=None, not_in_channel_id=None) -> List[Dict]`
`POST /users/search` — body: `{team_id, term, in_channel_id?, not_in_channel_id?}`.

children.title: Teams + Channels
children.content:
### `async list_teams(*, page=0, per_page=60, include_total_count=False) -> List[Dict]`
`GET /teams`.

### `async get_team(team_id) -> Dict`
`GET /teams/{team_id}`.

### `async list_channels(team_id, *, page=0, per_page=60, include_deleted=False) -> List[Dict]`
`GET /teams/{team_id}/channels`.

### `async get_channel(channel_id) -> Dict`
`GET /channels/{channel_id}`.

### `async create_channel(team_id, name, display_name, *, type="O", purpose="", header="") -> Dict`
`POST /channels`. `type` must be `"O"` (open / public) or `"P"` (private) — anything else raises `ValueError` at the connector layer.

### `async delete_channel(channel_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /channels/{channel_id}` (server-side soft-delete).

### `async add_user_to_channel(channel_id, user_id) -> Dict`
`POST /channels/{channel_id}/members`.

children.title: Posts + Files
children.content:
### `async post_message(channel_id, message, *, root_id=None, props=None, file_ids=None) -> Dict`
`POST /posts`. Threaded replies via `root_id`.

### `async get_post(post_id) -> Dict`
`GET /posts/{post_id}`.

### `async update_post(post_id, *, message=None, props=None) -> Dict`
`PUT /posts/{post_id}` — partial update.

### `async delete_post(post_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /posts/{post_id}`.

### `async list_channel_posts(channel_id, *, page=0, per_page=60, since=None, before=None, after=None) -> Dict`
`GET /channels/{channel_id}/posts` — paginated channel timeline.

### `async upload_file(channel_id, file_bytes, filename) -> Dict`
`POST /files` (multipart/form-data).

### `async get_file_info(file_id) -> Dict`
`GET /files/{file_id}/info`.

children.title: Webhooks · Bots · Commands
children.content:
### `async create_incoming_webhook(channel_id, display_name, description="", *, username=None, icon_url=None) -> Dict`
`POST /hooks/incoming`.

### `async list_incoming_webhooks(*, team_id=None, page=0, per_page=60) -> List[Dict]`
`GET /hooks/incoming`.

### `async create_outgoing_webhook(team_id, display_name, trigger_words, callback_urls, *, channel_id=None, description="", content_type="application/x-www-form-urlencoded") -> Dict`
`POST /hooks/outgoing`.

### `async list_outgoing_webhooks(*, team_id=None, page=0, per_page=60) -> List[Dict]`
`GET /hooks/outgoing`.

### `async list_bots(*, page=0, per_page=60, include_deleted=False) -> List[Dict]`
`GET /bots`.

### `async list_team_commands(team_id, *, custom_only=False) -> List[Dict]`
`GET /commands?team_id=…`.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
MattermostError                            # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── MattermostAuthError                    # 401 / 403
├── MattermostBadRequestError              # 400
├── MattermostNotFoundError                # 404
├── MattermostConflictError                # 409
├── MattermostRateLimitError               # 429 — retry_after_s
└── MattermostServerError                  # 5xx
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
MattermostNetworkError = MattermostServerError    # legacy name from older code
MattermostNotFound     = MattermostNotFoundError
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 409 | Raise immediately |
| 429 | Exponential backoff `min(0.5 * 2^attempt + jitter, 16s)`, honours `Retry-After` / `X-RateLimit-Reset` headers, retries up to `_max_retries=3` |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff, retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.NetworkError` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `MattermostServerError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403 → ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
404 → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,   FAILED)            — wrong server_url
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)         — logged as warning
other → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `server_url` or `personal_access_token` blank | Re-enter both in ACP. Mattermost shows the token only once at generation — rotate if lost. |
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at install | 401 from `/users/me` — token rejected | Re-mint the PAT in **Account Settings → Security → Personal Access Tokens**. Verify the system admin has enabled PATs in System Console. |
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, FAILED)` at health_check ("endpoint not found") | `/system/ping` returned 404 — wrong server URL | Verify the server URL points to the Mattermost root (no `/api/v4` suffix). The connector normalizes it but a wrong host fails fast. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | 401 — token revoked or system admin disabled PATs | Re-mint the PAT and update the connector. |
| `mattermost.http.retry` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (server rate cap, default ~10 req/sec per token) | The client retries with exponential backoff respecting `Retry-After`. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` or batch fewer requests. |
| 404 on a channel-scoped call | Bad channel/team/post ID, or the token's user lacks access | Verify the IDs and that the PAT's user is a member of the target team/channel. |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'MattermostNetworkError'` | Older `exceptions.py` without back-compat aliases | Already fixed — `MattermostNetworkError = MattermostServerError` alias added. |
| Multipart upload returns 413 | File exceeds server `MaxFileSize` (default 50 MB) | Raise the limit in System Console → File Storage or split the file. |
| `create_channel` returns 400 with "invalid channel type" | `type` not one of `"O"` / `"P"` | The connector pre-validates this and raises `ValueError` before the HTTP call. |
